text: Whatever is in the heavens and the earth glorifies Allah, for He is the Almighty, All-Wise.
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He gives life and causes death. And He is Most Capable of everything.
He is the First and the Last, the Most High and Most Near, <sup foot_note=1167>1167</sup> and He has ňĻperfectňļ knowledge of all things.

foot_notes.1167: Another possible translation: ‚Äúthe Manifest ňĻthrough His signsňļ and the Hidden ňĻfrom His creationňļ.‚ÄĚ

text: He is the One Who created the heavens and the earth in six Days, <sup foot_note=1168>1168</sup> then established Himself on the Throne. He knows whatever goes into the earth and whatever comes out of it, and whatever descends from the sky and whatever ascends into it. And He is with you wherever you are. <sup foot_note=1169>1169</sup> For Allah is All-Seeing of what you do.
To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. And to Allah all matters are returned.
He merges the night into day and the day into night. And He knows best what is ňĻhiddenňļ in the heart.
Believe in Allah and His Messenger, and donate from what He has entrusted you with. So those of you who believe and donate will have a mighty reward.
Why do you not believe in Allah while the Messenger is inviting you to have faith in your Lord, although He has already taken your covenant, <sup foot_note=1170>1170</sup> if you will ever believe.
He is the One Who sends down clear revelations to His servant to bring you out of darkness and into light. For indeed Allah is Ever Gracious and Most Merciful to you.

And why should you not spend in the cause of Allah, while Allah is the ňĻsoleňļ inheritor of the heavens and the earth? Those of you who donated and fought before the victory ňĻover Meccaňļ are unparalleled. They are far greater in rank than those who donated and fought afterwards. <sup foot_note=1171>1171</sup> Yet Allah has promised each a fine reward. And Allah is All-Aware of what you do.

foot_notes.1171: Prior to the victory over Mecca, Muslims were outnumbered and perceived as weak. Once Mecca surrendered to the Muslims, many tribes either accepted Islam or made peace agreements with the Muslim victors. Therefore, the believers who donated and strived at the time of hardship and weakness deserve more rewards than those who did so at the time of ease and prominence.

text: Who is it that will lend to Allah a good loan which Allah will multiply ňĻmany times overňļ for them, and they will have an honourable reward? <sup foot_note=1172>1172</sup>

On that Day you will see believing men and women with their light shining ahead of them and on their right. ňĻThey will be told,ňļ ‚ÄúToday you have good news of Gardens, under which rivers flow, ňĻfor youňļ to stay in forever. This is ňĻtrulyňļ the ultimate triumph.‚ÄĚ

On that Day hypocrite men and women will beg the believers, ‚ÄúWait for us so that we may have some of your light.‚ÄĚ It will be said ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúGo back ňĻto the worldňļ and seek a light ňĻthereňļ!‚ÄĚ Then a ňĻseparatingňļ wall with a gate will be erected between them. On the near side will be grace and on the far side will be torment. <sup foot_note=1173>1173</sup>

foot_notes.1173: This wall is said to be the barrier of Al-A‚Äôr√Ęf (or the heights) between Paradise and Hell. See 7:46-49.

text: The tormented will cry out to those graced, ‚ÄúWere we not with you?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúYes ňĻyou wereňļ. But you chose to be tempted ňĻby hypocrisyňļ, ňĻeagerlyňļ awaited ňĻour demiseňļ, doubted ňĻthe truthňļ, and were deluded by false hopes until Allah‚Äôs decree ňĻof your deathňļ came to pass. And ňĻsoňļ the Chief Deceiver <sup foot_note=1174>1174</sup> deceived you about Allah.
So Today no ransom will be accepted from you ňĻhypocritesňļ, nor from the disbelievers. Your home is the Fire‚ÄĒit is the ňĻonlyňļ fitting place for you. What an evil destination!‚ÄĚ

Has the time not yet come for believers‚Äô hearts to be humbled at the remembrance of Allah and what has been revealed of the truth, and not be like those given the Scripture before‚ÄĒňĻthoseňļ who were spoiled for so long that their hearts became hardened. And many of them are ňĻstillňļ rebellious.
Know that Allah revives the earth after its death. <sup foot_note=1175>1175</sup> We have certainly made the signs clear for you so perhaps you will understand.

foot_notes.1175: So it is easy for Him to soften your hearts as well.

text: Indeed, those men and women who give in charity and lend to Allah a good loan will have it multiplied for them, and they will have an honourable reward. <sup foot_note=1176>1176</sup>

ňĻAs forňļ those who believe in Allah and His messengers, it is they who are ňĻtrulyňļ the people of truth. And the martyrs, with their Lord, will have their reward and their light. But ňĻas forňļ those who disbelieve and reject Our signs, it is they who will be the residents of the Hellfire.

Know that this worldly life is no more than play, amusement, luxury, mutual boasting, and competition in wealth and children. This is like rain that causes plants to grow, to the delight of the planters. But later the plants dry up and you see them wither, then they are reduced to chaff. And in the Hereafter there will be either severe punishment or forgiveness and pleasure of Allah, whereas the life of this world is no more than the delusion of enjoyment.

ňĻSoňļ compete with one another for forgiveness from your Lord and a Paradise as vast as the heavens and the earth, prepared for those who believe in Allah and His messengers. This is the favour of Allah. He grants it to whoever He wills. And Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.
No calamity ňĻor blessingňļ occurs on earth or in yourselves without being ňĻwrittenňļ in a Record before We bring it into being. This is certainly easy for Allah.
ňĻWe let you know thisňļ so that you neither grieve over what you have missed nor boast over what He has granted you. For Allah does not like whoever is arrogant, boastful‚ÄĒ
those who are stingy and promote stinginess among people. And whoever turns away ňĻshould know thatňļ Allah ňĻaloneňļ is truly the Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.

Indeed, We sent Our messengers with clear proofs, and with them We sent down the Scripture and the balance ňĻof justiceňļ so that people may administer justice. And We sent down iron with its great might, benefits for humanity, and means for Allah to prove who ňĻis willing toňļ stand up for Him and His messengers without seeing Him. Surely Allah is All-Powerful, Almighty.
And indeed, We sent Noah and Abraham and reserved prophethood and revelation for their descendants. Some of them are ňĻrightlyňļ guided, while most are rebellious.

Then in the footsteps of these ňĻprophetsňļ, We sent Our messengers, and ňĻafter themňļ We sent Jesus, son of Mary, and granted him the Gospel, and instilled compassion and mercy into the hearts of his followers. As for monasticism, they made it up‚ÄĒWe never ordained it for them‚ÄĒonly seeking to please Allah, yet they did not ňĻevenňļ observe it strictly. So We rewarded those of them who were faithful. But most of them are rebellious.

O people of faith! Fear Allah and believe in His Messenger. ňĻAndňļ He will grant you a double share of His mercy, provide you with a light to walk in ňĻon Judgment Dayňļ, and forgive you. For Allah is All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

ňĻThis is soňļ that the People of the Book ňĻwho deny the Prophetňļ may know that they do not have any control over Allah‚Äôs grace, and that all grace is in Allah‚Äôs Hands. He grants it to whoever He wills. For Allah is the Lord of infinite bounty.
